papers.title: ActionChunk: Temporal Action Chunking for Robust Long-Horizon Manipulation
papers.abstract: ActionChunk improves robust long-horizon robot manipulation by predicting temporally coherent action sequences instead of isolated actions.

sections.title: Method
sections.text: The policy predicts action chunks containing eight temporally coherent actions. Chunk overlap and temporal ensembling reduce compounding control errors during long-horizon manipulation.

sections.title: Experiments
sections.text: On the RoboSuite LongBench tasks, action chunking improves success rate by 12 percentage points over single-step behavior cloning.

sections.title: Limitations
sections.text: Fixed chunk length can be inefficient when a task alternates between fast contact events and slow free-space motion.

papers.title: Diffusion-VLA: Robot Control with a Conditional Diffusion Action Expert
papers.abstract: Diffusion-VLA combines self-generated reasoning with a conditional diffusion action expert for generalizable robot control.

sections.title: Architecture
sections.text: A vision-language backbone produces self-generated reasoning tokens. A conditional diffusion action expert then denoises continuous robot action trajectories conditioned on those tokens.

sections.title: Evaluation
sections.text: The model is evaluated on the LIBERO benchmark and improves average task success by eight percentage points over the autoregressive baseline.

sections.title: Ablation
sections.text: Removing self-generated reasoning reduces performance on spatially ambiguous instructions.

papers.title: HAMSTER: Hierarchical Action Models for Open-World Robot Manipulation
papers.abstract: HAMSTER separates high-level spatial planning from low-level motor control for open-world long-horizon manipulation.

sections.title: Hierarchical Method
sections.text: The high-level model predicts a two-dimensional end-effector trajectory over the image. A low-level policy grounds the 2D trajectory into closed-loop robot actions.

sections.title: Long-Horizon Results
sections.text: Hierarchical planning improves completion of multi-stage kitchen and tabletop tasks, especially when intermediate goals are far apart.

sections.title: Limitations
sections.text: The two-dimensional plan can lose depth information in cluttered scenes.

papers.title: X-VLA: Soft-Prompted Cross-Embodiment Vision-Language-Action Learning
papers.abstract: X-VLA uses embodiment-specific soft prompts and bridge data to scale a shared VLA transformer across robot platforms.

sections.title: Cross-Embodiment Method
sections.text: A shared transformer is conditioned by a learned soft prompt for each robot embodiment. Small bridge datasets align action spaces without changing the shared backbone.

sections.title: Transfer Results
sections.text: Soft prompting enables zero-shot instruction transfer and few-shot adaptation to a new robot platform.

sections.title: Limitations
sections.text: A new embodiment still needs calibrated bridge data when its action space differs substantially.

papers.title: SmolVLA: An Affordable Vision-Language-Action Model for Efficient Robotics
papers.abstract: SmolVLA is a compact 450M parameter policy designed for affordable robotics and consumer hardware.

sections.title: Efficiency
sections.text: The 450M parameter model runs on a consumer GPU. Asynchronous inference overlaps visual encoding with robot execution to reduce control latency.

sections.title: Results
sections.text: SmolVLA retains competitive manipulation success while using substantially less memory than multi-billion-parameter baselines.

sections.title: Limitations
sections.text: The compact model is weaker on rare visual concepts that require broad world knowledge.

papers.title: DexCap: Portable Motion Capture for Dexterous Robot Demonstrations
papers.abstract: DexCap provides a portable motion capture system for collecting dexterous manipulation demonstrations outside a robot laboratory.

sections.title: Capture System
sections.text: The operator wears instrumented gloves while portable RGB-D cameras estimate hand and object poses. The pipeline retargets the captured motion to a dexterous robot hand.

sections.title: Dataset
sections.text: The system collects scalable in-the-wild demonstrations for tool use, object handover, and articulated object manipulation.

sections.title: Limitations
sections.text: Severe hand-object occlusion can reduce pose reconstruction accuracy.

papers.title: RT-2: Vision-Language-Action Models Transfer Web Knowledge to Robotic Control
papers.abstract: RT-2 transfers semantic concepts learned from web-scale vision-language data into end-to-end robotic control.

sections.title: Co-Fine-Tuning
sections.text: RT-2 co-fine-tunes web vision-language data and robot trajectories. Robot actions are represented as text tokens in the same vocabulary used by the vision-language model.

sections.title: Emergent Skills
sections.text: Web knowledge transfer improves semantic reasoning, symbol understanding, and novel object generalization in robotic control tasks.

sections.title: Limitations
sections.text: Large-model inference cost limits high-frequency control and on-device deployment.

papers.title: Vision-Language-Action Models: Progress, Applications, and Open Challenges
papers.abstract: This survey reviews VLA architectures, datasets, applications, evaluation protocols, and open research challenges.

sections.title: Open Challenges
sections.text: The main open challenges are safety guarantees, data diversity, long-horizon evaluation, reproducibility, and robustness under distribution shift.

sections.title: Evaluation
sections.text: The field lacks standardized evaluation protocols that jointly measure task success, semantic generalization, latency, and safe failure behavior.

sections.title: Research Directions
sections.text: Future systems should combine grounded reasoning, efficient adaptation, uncertainty estimation, and auditable evidence.

knowledge_bases.name: Empty Scope
knowledge_bases.description: Empty knowledge base for negative tests
